common.betaTooltip: This platform is still in development and may have bugs. Use with caution.

home.metaDesc: Free, open-source OWASP Top 10 scanner for websites and code repositories. Security score out of 100 with detailed per-category findings.
home.headline: Free OWASP Top 10 scanner for websites and code repositories
home.description: Verify ownership, run passive and active security checks, and get a detailed score mapped to all 10 OWASP categories. Free, open source, no tracking.
home.feature1Desc: Before any scan, you must prove you own the domain or repository. No one can scan a project they don't control.
home.feature2Desc: HTTP security headers, SSL/TLS config, OWASP ZAP active attack simulation, and optional static source-code analysis.
home.feature3Desc: Every finding maps to a specific OWASP Top 10 category. See what passed, what failed, and exactly what was — and wasn't — checked.
home.howItWorksSubtitle: Three steps to your security score
home.step1Desc: Enter a website URL or link a code repository on GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket.
home.step2Desc: Confirm domain ownership via DNS TXT record, HTML meta tag, or .well-known file. For repositories, a single file commit is enough.
home.step3Desc: OWMeter runs checks across all 10 OWASP categories and returns a score out of 100 with per-category findings.
home.owaspTitle: Every check maps to the OWASP Top 10
home.owaspSubtitle: The OWASP Top 10 is the industry standard for the most critical web security risks. OWMeter evaluates your project against each one.
home.openSourceDesc: No paid plans, no vendor lock-in. OWMeter is open source — inspect the code, contribute, or self-host.
home.showcaseSubtitle: Sites scoring 90 or above on OWMeter
home.showcaseEmpty: Be the first to score 90 or above and appear here.

login.title: Sign in to OWMeter
login.subtitle: Scan your projects against the OWASP Top 10
login.terms: We only request read access to your email.

dashboard.title: My Projects
dashboard.subtitle: Add a project, verify ownership, then scan it against OWASP Top 10.
dashboard.noProjects: No projects yet. Add one above to get started.
dashboard.selectProjectType: What would you like to add?
dashboard.typeWebsiteDesc: For published websites. Requires domain ownership verification.
dashboard.typeCodeRepoDesc: For libraries and projects not published on a domain. Requires repository ownership verification (GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket).
dashboard.codeRepoJsTsOnlyDesc: Source code analysis currently supports JavaScript and TypeScript projects (package.json). Java, .NET, Python and other languages will be detected but their dependencies and code patterns will not be analyzed.
dashboard.domainAlreadyInList: This domain is already in your list.
dashboard.domainClaimedByOther: This domain has already been verified by another user.

site.noScans: No analyses yet. Click "New Scan" to begin.
site.deleteConfirm: Delete this project and all its scans?
site.deleteError: Could not delete the project. Please try again.

verify.title: Verify ownership of {domain}
verify.subtitle: Choose a verification method and follow the instructions.
verify.failed: Verification failed. Make sure the token is correctly placed and try again.

verifyRepo.subtitleRequired: Verify ownership of your repository for a complete analysis. Supported: GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket.
verifyRepo.subtitleOptional: Verify ownership of your repository (GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket) to enable full code analysis.
verifyRepo.publicOnly: Only public repositories are supported for verification.
verifyRepo.zipAlternative: If your repository is private, you can upload a ZIP of your code when starting an analysis.
verifyRepo.instructions: Create a file named .owmeter at the root of your repository with this exact content:
verifyRepo.fileHint: Commit the file, then enter your repository URL below and click Verify.
verifyRepo.failed: Verification failed. Make sure the .owmeter file exists with the correct content.

scan.subtitle: OWASP Top 10 scan
scan.noScans: No scans yet. Click "Start Scan" to begin.
scan.running: Scan in progress… this may take a few minutes.
scan.newScanDesc: Run a new OWASP Top 10 scan against this project.
scan.scanTypePASSIVEDesc: Header analysis + OWASP ZAP active scan of the live site. Code-only categories shown as not evaluated.
scan.scanTypeFULLDesc: Everything in Passive, plus static code analysis covering all 10 OWASP categories.
scan.scanTypeCODEDesc: Static analysis of your repository covering all applicable OWASP categories.
scan.unverifiedSourceDesc: Code was provided via ZIP upload. Repository ownership was not verified.
scan.zipUploadHint: Upload a ZIP archive of your source code (max 50 MB)
scan.zipRequired: Please select a ZIP file before starting.
scan.zipTooLarge: ZIP file exceeds the 50 MB limit.
scan.zipNotVerified: The ZIP could not be verified as belonging to this domain. Upload the source code of the verified site.
scan.zipNoValidCode: The ZIP does not contain any recognizable source code files. Please upload a valid code repository.
scan.privacyDesc: Source code is processed entirely in memory and discarded immediately after analysis. It is never written to disk, logged, or persisted in any form.
scan.codeNotCheckedBanner: Passive scan — code-only categories were not evaluated.

prompt.modalSubtitle: Copy this prompt and paste it into your AI assistant.

scan.partialEvaluatedDesc: Partially evaluated — server aspects (TLS, cookie flags) require a live domain
scan.falsePositiveHint: Think one of these findings might be a false positive? Help us improve the scanner — open an issue with the finding title and the scanned URL.
scan.failedDesc: The scan could not be completed. Check the reason below and try again once the issue is resolved.
scan.invalidDesc: This scan result is no longer valid.

partialNotes.PASSIVE_A01_BROKEN_ACCESS_CONTROL: ZAP checks path traversal and sensitive paths. Verifying RBAC, IDOR and auth middleware requires source code.
partialNotes.PASSIVE_A02_CRYPTOGRAPHIC_FAILURES: Checks HTTPS, HSTS, TLS config and cookie Secure flag. Verifying crypto algorithm choices (MD5, SHA-1, JWT) requires source code.
partialNotes.PASSIVE_A03_INJECTION: ZAP fires SQL/XSS payloads on public endpoints. Full injection coverage (parameterized queries on all code paths) requires source code.
partialNotes.PASSIVE_A07_AUTH_FAILURES: Checks cookie flags and ZAP tests brute-force/session. Verifying JWT secret strength and password hashing requires source code.
partialNotes.PASSIVE_A06_VULNERABLE_COMPONENTS: ZAP retire.js detects vulnerable client-side JS libraries. Verifying server-side dependencies (package.json, lock files) requires source code.
partialNotes.PASSIVE_A10_SSRF: ZAP sends SSRF probes to discovered inputs. Verifying every internal URL fetch validates its input requires source code.
partialNotes.CODE_A01_BROKEN_ACCESS_CONTROL: Code checks CORS wildcard patterns. Verifying path traversal, IDOR and missing auth on routes requires a live domain.
partialNotes.CODE_A02_CRYPTOGRAPHIC_FAILURES: Code checks weak algorithms (MD5, SHA-1) and JWT config. Verifying TLS and HTTPS enforcement requires a live domain.
partialNotes.CODE_A07_AUTH_FAILURES: Code checks JWT signing and bcrypt usage. Verifying cookie flags (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite) requires a live domain.

apiKey.title: CI/CD Integration
apiKey.description: Trigger scans automatically from your CI/CD pipeline using this project API key.
apiKey.regenerateConfirm: This will invalidate the current key. Are you sure?
apiKey.badgeDescription: Add this badge to your README to display the security score of your project.

footer.tagline: Free OWASP Top 10 scanner for websites and repositories.

legalPrivacy.title: Privacy Policy
legalPrivacy.s1Body: Your email address and display name, provided by Google or GitHub when you sign in. The website URLs and repository links you add to your projects. Security scan results generated for your verified projects.
legalPrivacy.s2Body: To authenticate you, maintain your session, and run OWASP Top 10 security scans on your verified projects. We do not sell, share, or use your data for advertising or third-party analytics.
legalPrivacy.s3Body: Access to source code is exclusively for analysis purposes, processed entirely in memory and discarded immediately after the scan completes. It is never written to disk, logged, or stored in any form.
legalPrivacy.s4Body: We use a single session cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict) for authentication. We do not use tracking cookies or advertising cookies of any kind.
legalPrivacy.s5Body: Google and GitHub OAuth are used for authentication only. We receive your email address and display name. No other third-party services receive your personal data.
legalPrivacy.s6Body: You can delete your projects and all associated scan results from the dashboard at any time. To delete your account entirely, open an issue on our GitHub repository.
legalPrivacy.s7Body: OWMeter is open source. You can inspect how your data is handled in the public repository and verify these claims directly in the code.

legalTerms.title: Terms of Service
legalTerms.s1Body: You may only scan websites or repositories that you own or have explicit written permission to scan. Domain and repository ownership verification is required before any scan is performed — this is enforced by the platform. Using OWMeter to scan systems you do not control is strictly prohibited.
legalTerms.s2Body: OWMeter is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind, express or implied. Scan results are informational only and do not guarantee complete security coverage or the absence of vulnerabilities.
legalTerms.s3Body: To the fullest extent permitted by law, we are not liable for any direct, indirect, or consequential damages arising from your use of OWMeter or from acting on scan results.
legalTerms.s4Body: OWMeter is open source software. You may inspect, fork, and self-host it under the terms of its open source licence. The hosted service at owmeter.dev is free to use.
legalTerms.s5Body: We may update these terms at any time. Continued use of the service after changes are posted implies acceptance of the updated terms.

privacy.isPublicLabel: Allow this project to appear publicly when it achieves a score of 90 or above
privacy.isPublicHint: Sites scoring 90 or above may appear in the home page showcase. You can change this at any time.
privacy.saveError: Could not save. Please try again.
